    return "Not a date."

    import calendar

        "%d %b %Y", "%d-%b-%Y", "%d/%b/%Y", "%d %b %y", "%d %B %Y", "%d %B %y",
        "%d %b %Y", "%d %b %y", "%d %B %Y", "%d %B %y", "%d-%b-%y", "%d/%b/%y"
    ]
    
    for fmt in formats:
        try:
            dt = datetime.datetime.strptime(date_str, fmt)
            break
        except ValueError:
            continue
    
    # If parsing failed, try to extract month and year from the string

        # Extract month name and year
        match = re.search(r'([A-Z][a-z]{2})\s(\d{2})', date_str)
        if match:
            month_name = match.group(1)
            year = match.group(2)
            return f"{month_name} {year}"
        
        # Extract month abbreviation and year (two-digit)
        match = re.search(r'([A-Z][a-z]{2})\s(\d{2})', date_str)
        if match:
            month_abbr = match.group(1)
            year = match.group(2)
            return f"{month_abbr} {year}"
        
        # Extract month abbreviation and two-digit year
        match = re.search(r'([A-Z][a-z]{2})\s(\d{2})', date_str)
        if match:
            month_abbr = match.group(1)
            year = match.group(2)
            return f"{month_abbr} {year}"
    
    # Extract month abbreviation and year from parsed datetime
    month_abbr = calendar.month_abbr[dt.month]
    year = dt.year % 100  # Extract two-digit year
    return f"{month_abbr} {year}"
